task_completion.description: Log in to the SEB Server
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SEB Server dashboard or interface is visible, indicating a successful login (e.g., 'super-admin' profile visible).

task_completion.description: Create a new Connection Configuration with the specified name
task_completion.visual_evidence: A form or list showing a Connection Configuration named exactly 'Campus Lockdown Browser Config'.
The password field in the configuration form is filled (even if masked), or the configuration summary shows a password has been set.
A checkbox, toggle, or radio button labeled 'With Fallback' is visibly checked or enabled in the configuration settings.
The URL 'https://exam.university.edu/login' is entered into the fallback start URL text field.
The configuration 'Campus Lockdown Browser Config' is shown in a list with an 'Active' status, or a toggle/button for activation is visibly turned on.

integrity.description: Agent used the web GUI to configure the server
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser interacting with the SEB Server web interface, not terminal commands (like curl) or API testing tools.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual SEB Server application
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows 'localhost:8080' and the standard SEB Server UI is present, rather than a text editor or mock HTML file.

integrity.description: Agent did not bypass the application by editing the database directly
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no screenshots of database management tools (like pgAdmin, DBeaver, or sqlite3 terminal) being used to insert the configuration.
